parts.answer: I < 1 A to at least 2 decimal places with unit V values increasing 1 R values correct 1 All R given to either 2 or either 3 significant figures 1 suitable scales 1 all plots correct to ½ small square 1 good line judgement, thin, continuous line 1 triangle using at least ½ of candidate’s line between extreme plots. 1 October/November 2023 Question Answer Marks temperatures decreasing 1  correct  S correct correct values and  T <  S justification matches statement with numbers correctly quoted 1 for at least another 180s/until the temperature is close to room temperature or stops decreasing 1 October/November 2023 Question Answer Marks 4 MP1 labelled diagram diagram with lens between object and screen, and filter anywhere between the illuminated object and the lens or between the source of illumination and the object, with a least 1 label MP2 method move object / lens / screen to obtain focused / sharp / clear image MP3 measure / record / find / calculate / note u and v 1 MP4 calculate / determine / find the focal length 1 MP5 repeat with different colours / filters 1 MP6 table table with clear columns for (filter) colour, u and v AND with appropriate units MP7 how to reach conclusion plot bar chart of focal length and colour OR compare results to see if / how the change of colour affects the focal length OR compare results to determine the effect of a change in colour on the focal length Additional graph notes: NOTE: – The principle to apply here is ‘could I draw a significantly better line, using these points, under examination conditions?’ If the answer is definitely ‘yes’, do not award the mark. NOTE: – If candidate’s scale consists of actual readings at equal intervals this will produce a perfect straight line! The only marks available in this case are the first (axes right way round and labelled) So maximum 1. If axes are wrong way round, the other 3 marks are still available.
